_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-1 Adult/Pediatric Trauma Triage (Revised 12/16/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-1: Adult/Pediatric Trauma Triage — MDHHS Approved 12/16/22, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Facilitate delivery of the patient to the most appropriate level of care in the most expeditious manner. Exception: trauma patients requiring airway intervention that cannot be accomplished by pre-hospital personnel are transported to the closest appropriate hospital for airway management, resuscitation, and immediate transfer for definitive care.

sections.title: RED CRITERIA — High Risk for Serious Injury
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-1: Trauma Triage — Section I.A
points: INJURY PATTERNS: Penetrating injuries to head, neck, torso, and proximal extremities; skull deformity or suspected skull fracture; suspected spinal injury with new motor or sensory loss; chest wall instability, deformity, or suspected flail chest; suspected pelvic fracture; suspected fracture of two or more proximal long bones; crushed, degloved, mangled, or pulseless extremity; amputation proximal to wrist or ankle; active bleeding requiring a tourniquet or wound packing with continuous pressure
MENTAL STATUS & VITAL SIGNS — ALL PATIENTS: Unable to follow commands (motor GCS < 6); RR < 10 or > 29 breaths/min; respiratory distress or need for respiratory support; room-air pulse oximetry < 90%
MENTAL STATUS & VITAL SIGNS — AGE 0–9 YEARS: SBP < 70 mmHg + (2 × age in years)
MENTAL STATUS & VITAL SIGNS — AGE 10–64 YEARS: SBP < 90 mmHg OR HR > SBP
MENTAL STATUS & VITAL SIGNS — AGE ≥ 65 YEARS: SBP < 110 mmHg OR HR > SBP

sections.title: RED CRITERIA — Transport Destinations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-1: Trauma Triage — Section I.B
points: ADULT (≥ 15 years of age) — order of destination preference: (1) Level 1 or Level 2 Trauma Center within 45 minutes — if not possible by ground within 45 min, consider air medical; (2) Level 3 Trauma Center within 45 minutes; (3) Level 4 Trauma Center within 45 minutes

PEDIATRICS (≤ 14 years of age) — order of destination preference: (1) Pediatric Level 1 or Level 2 Trauma Center within 45 minutes; (2) Level 1 or Level 2 Trauma Center within 45 minutes — if neither peds nor adult Level 1/2 possible by ground, consider air medical; (3) Level 3 Trauma Center within 45 minutes; (4) Level 4 Trauma Center within 45 minutes

sections.title: YELLOW CRITERIA — Moderate Risk for Serious Injury
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 2-1: Trauma Triage — Section II
points: MECHANISM OF INJURY — High-Risk Auto Crash: partial or complete ejection; significant intrusion (including roof) — > 12 inches occupant site OR > 18 inches any site OR need for extrication for entrapped patient; death in passenger compartment; child (age 0–9 years) unrestrained or in unsecured child safety seat; vehicle telemetry data consistent with severe injury

MECHANISM OF INJURY — Other: rider separated from transport vehicle with significant impact (motorcycle, ATV, horse, etc.); pedestrian/bicycle rider thrown, run over, or with significant impact; fall from height > 10 feet

EMS JUDGEMENT — Consider risk factors: low-level falls in young children (age ≤ 5 years) or older adults (age ≥ 65 years) with significant head impact; anticoagulant use; suspicion of child abuse; special, high-resource healthcare needs; pregnancy > 20 weeks; burns in conjunction with trauma; children should be triaged preferentially to pediatric capable centers

Patients meeting YELLOW CRITERIA WHO DO NOT MEET RED CRITERIA should be preferentially transported to a trauma center, as available within the geographic constraints of the regional trauma system (need not be the highest-level trauma center per local MCA and trauma policies)

sections.title: Notes
points: Medical Control may be contacted to determine the appropriate destination when indicated
High risk pelvic fracture does NOT include isolated hip fractures without significant mechanism

deterioration_flags: Any RED CRITERIA met — transport immediately to highest appropriate trauma center within 45 minutes
YELLOW CRITERIA without RED — transport preferentially to trauma center per regional system availability
Pediatric patients — triage preferentially to pediatric capable centers when possible
Airway compromise not manageable pre-hospital — transport to closest appropriate facility for airway management and transfer
scope_notes: Transport destination decisions: all levels — contact Medical Control when destination is unclear
